rationale: The cited article ("Extending Discrete Choice Models to Incorporate Attitudinal and Other Latent Variables") develops joint measurement-choice latent-variable modeling, which closely aligns with the citing paper’s core contribution of an HB structural equation model linking latent subjective characteristics to purchase intention. This strong methodological and construct overlap (latent attitudes/perceptions, measurement error, discrete choice/purchase modeling) makes the citation plausibly substantive rather than a generic background mention.
